model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed-step evaluation (256 optimizer steps), increasing batch size should expose more unique training examples within the same step budget, often improving early-loss quality for this MLP workload. A modest LR increase keeps update magnitude aligned with the larger batch while remaining conservative for Adam stability.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The previous LR schedule change regressed, so disabling it restores the stronger constant-LR regime. Switching from Adam to AdamW is a minimal optimizer-family change that often improves MLP generalization with the same step budget and existing weight decay setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With AdamW and larger batch under a short fixed-step budget, a slightly higher learning rate can improve early optimization progress and reduce validation loss within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step mode, doubling batch size increases sample coverage per optimizer step, likely improving validation loss for this undertrained MLP by exposing more of the dataset within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After a large gain from increasing batch size to 256, raising LR can better match the lower gradient noise and speed progress within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase to 1.5e-3 regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 returns to the stronger operating point before testing further changes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 update steps, this MLP is likely optimization-limited more than overfitting-limited; removing weight decay can improve fit quality and lower validation loss within the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Keeping both hidden layers at the same width reduces expansion-induced optimization difficulty while preserving depth, which can improve loss under a short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With a strict 256-step budget, a slightly narrower MLP can be easier to optimize and may yield lower validation loss than the current width while keeping the same overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The width reduction regressed substantially, so reverting to the prior width restores the stronger architecture baseline before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"delete_exact","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)\n","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"For AdamW on this small MLP, gradient clipping may be unnecessarily constraining updates under a short fixed-step budget; removing it can improve optimization speed and lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given fixed-step training, a larger batch can further improve gradient quality and dataset coverage per update; this may reduce validation loss beyond the current 256-batch setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After a major gain from batch 512, a modest LR increase may better match the reduced gradient noise and improve optimization progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step mode, increasing batch size further raises sample throughput per optimizer step and can improve loss for this stable AdamW setup; this is a direct continuation of the strong gains from 256 to 512.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 1024, a higher learning rate is often needed to fully leverage low-noise gradients; increasing LR should improve convergence within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
